channel.title: Category Theory on Elliot's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Category Theory on Elliot's Blog

item.title: Products, Exponentials, and Adjoint Functors
item.description: In category theory, you often think something is a certain type of thing but actually it turns out to also be a different type of thing. In a first introduction, it appears that functors are somehow one level higher than morphisms, because functors map morphisms to morphisms. Similarly, natural transformations should be one level higher than functors because they map functors to functors. This is true if you want to be closed-minded and sad. But it ignores a beautiful thing about category theory: it is so abstract and general that it can do category theory about itself.
